etymology.description: The word 時間(時間) (shíjiān, "time") is composed of two morphemes: 時(時) (shí, "time; season; hour; era") and 間(間) (jiān, "between; space; room; interval"). In combination, it designates the abstract concept of time as a continuum by which events are sequenced.
etymology.interpretation: 時(時) (shí, "time; season; hour; era") indicates the specific or cyclical nature of time, like hours or seasons; 間(間) (jiān, "between; space; room; interval") introduces the concept of a measured span or interval. Together, 時間(時間) (shíjiān) embraces the perception of time as segmented and measurable, like intervals between events, which is essential to understanding history and planning for the future.

examples.chinese: 时间过得真快(時間過得真快)。
我们需要更多的时间(我們需要更多的時間)，才能完成任务(才能完成任務)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào gèng duō de shíjiān, cái néng wánchéng rènwù.
examples.english: We need more time to complete the task.

examples.chinese: 现在是时候喝茶了(現在是時候喝茶了)。
examples.pinyin: Xiànzài shì shíhòu hē chá le.
examples.english: Now it's time to drink tea.
